yesterdayAnalysis: Monday's brick workout was executed as planned: 15.6mi bike ride (easy-moderate effort, though TSS wasn't recorded) followed by a 1-mile off-the-bike run at HR 137 (8 TSS). The run HR was appropriately controlled for an easy brick transition session. This represents the 14th brick workout of the build, maintaining critical race-day simulation work heading into the taper.
todayRecommendation: Execute the pool swim as prescribed—2,000m with 4x100 at race pace to maintain sharpness without overreaching. Then enjoy Run Club as an easy social run; keep HR below 140 and focus on loose, relaxed form rather than any intensity.
weekProgress: Week 12 of 13: Currently at 27 TSS of 200 target (14% complete) with 5 days remaining including today. This is exactly on-track for a taper week with a back-loaded schedule—the largest sessions (Wednesday bike+swim, Saturday OWS) are still ahead. Monday's brick is complete, today's swim/run combo will add meaningful volume, and the gradual wind-down through Thursday-Sunday protects freshness for race week.
keyInsight: You're 12 days out from IM 70.3 Oregon in peak taper mode—the focus now is maintaining sharpness and preserving energy, not accumulating TSS or fitness gains.
positives: Sleep quality rebounded to 78/100 last night with solid deep sleep (76min) and RHR back to 53bpm after the July 4th poor night
14 brick workouts completed through the build demonstrates excellent race-specific preparation
Yesterday's brick run HR of 137 shows good aerobic control and recovery capacity in the taper phase
